title: Just what are the mind rape scenes
display_title: Just what are the mind rape scenes
wikitext:
[]: Just what are the "mind rape" scenes?             Quote Post by alih92 » Thu Jan 21, 2010 8:32 pm I didn't see any rape >_>

[]:             Quote Post by Logan Payne » Thu Jan 21, 2010 8:41 pm If one would give an example, that would be most helpful.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Thu Jan 21, 2010 8:41 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Thu Jan 21, 2010 8:44 pm It wasn't "rape" per say. It was "mental infiltration" into Asuka's subconscious mind which brought up nasty memories of Asuka's childhood that she kept buried for so long. People just call it rape because they like to use dirty words to appease their fantasy bubbles.

[]:             Quote Post by Logan Payne » Thu Jan 21, 2010 8:44 pm See, I thought he was referring to any of the hell train scenes or the like.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jan 21, 2010 9:09 pm I may be mistaken, but I believe in the dub Grant actually says, "It's raping my mind!" Rape is basically defined as the forceful assault, infiltration, subjugation or submission without the consent of the second party. This may or may not be a sexual assault, but it is most commonly associated with it. Asuka's mind was literally raped in the terms that Arael infiltrated her mind without permission and subjugated her to relive her most painful memories. Other forms of mind rape within Evangelion are toward the viewer in the sense that it confuses the hell out of us and may or may not make us realize things about ourselves.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 21, 2010 9:18 pm Sachi wrote: I may be mistaken, but I believe in the dub Grant actually says, "It's raping my mind!"

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Thu Jan 21, 2010 9:44 pm Sachi wrote: I may be mistaken, but I believe in the dub Grant actually says, "It's raping my mind!"

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Thu Jan 21, 2010 9:44 pm Ironfoot wrote: People just call it rape because they like to use dirty words to appease their fantasy bubbles.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jan 21, 2010 10:21 pm via dictionary.com rape –noun 1. the unlawful compelling of a woman through physical force or duress to have sexual intercourse. 2. any act of sexual intercourse that is forced upon a person. 3. statutory rape. 4. an act of plunder, violent seizure, or abuse; despoliation; violation : the rape of the countryside. 5. Archaic. the act of seizing and carrying off by force.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Jan 21, 2010 10:45 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: The use of the term rape here isn't arbitrary as it has significant bearing on later events; notably in relation to Ritsuko's interrogation in #23' (which will eventually get a wiki page at some point)

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Thu Jan 21, 2010 10:50 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jan 21, 2010 11:21 pm Suggesting that all that happened to Ritsuko was being stripped naked presents a problem, because it in no way explains the extent of her breakdown at the end of #23'. For a woman who was so defiant during the interrogation itself, and so composed in general, her meltdown in Terminal Dogma seems rather incongruous in the light of a simple disrobing. Occam's razor will cut us here if we are not careful. Admittedly, we can only speculate on the nature of her offscreen interrogation, but it is clear that something deeply disturbing must have occurred to her in the interim. All the signs point towards a sexual assault, so this is generally taken to be the case.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Thu Jan 21, 2010 11:50 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jan 22, 2010 1:14 am Correct me if I'm wrong, but doesn't the literal translation of Seele's speech directly mention rape? Something about the word translated as 'disgrace' being synonymous with 'rape' in the original Japanese, I believe.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jan 22, 2010 1:19 am Angel-Rape happens with Asuka (the original ADV subs did say "Raping my mind"; the dub too) in 22 and Rei in 23. Ritsuko's regular rape is speculation, but likely considering her break-down and fact the word used for disgrace also translates into "rape". EDIT: Hah, crosspost with NemZ.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jan 25, 2010 3:28 pm The Risuko rape stuff was merged into its appropriate thread , so please keep this thread about the mental rape regarding the pilots. That is, if there is anything left to discuss regarding Asuka and Rei or the other pilots.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Mon Jan 25, 2010 3:50 pm It was me. I was rape.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Fri Feb 05, 2010 10:25 am I think Anno made it pretty clear that scene was a 'rape' in many ways, what with terms like "It hurts" "Don't come in" and the like flashing up. Hell, I've been fanwanking over the concept that pretty much every angel battle alluded to rape in a sense but that's unrelated =/

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Feb 05, 2010 3:28 pm Rather then rape, psychological desecration or audacious probing of a human's psyche are more adequate terms in Asuka's case.
